topic: Unit 5: A2 Themes (Young People in Society; Our Place in a Changing World)
subject: spanish

items.q: What goes between 'veo' and 'mi amigo' in 'I see my friend'?
items.why: Students omit the personal a. When the direct object is a SPECIFIC PERSON (or pet), Spanish inserts 'a' before it. Omitting it sounds odd. 'Veo a Juan' (not 'Veo Juan').

items.q: How do you say 'I am going to visit Spain' in Spanish?
items.why: Students drop the 'a' or use a conjugated verb. The near future is IR (conjugated) + A + INFINITIVE. The 'a' is obligatory: voy A comer, vamos A ver, vas A estudiar. Heavily tested for holiday/future plans.

items.q: When do you use the imperfect rather than the preterite?
items.correct: For ongoing, habitual, or descriptive past actions.
items.misconception: When the action happened a long time ago.
items.random_wrong: When you're unsure of the exact date.
items.why: Students choose tense by recency. The distinction is ASPECTUAL: preterite = completed/single ('comí' I ate); imperfect = ongoing/habitual ('comía' I used to eat / I was eating).

items.q: How do you say 'I am browsing the internet'?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: estoy navegando por internet. “navego por internet” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
The answer examiners want is: digital divide. “digital revolution” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year did the Spanish remote work law come into force?
items.why: The real figure is 2020. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
